title: Can Christopher Nolan's 'Tenet' Reopen Theaters?
format: Sean and Amanda work through whether Tenet can really reopen theaters in July 2020, and what a stalled release calendar does to the rest of the movie year.

segments.heading: Can 'Tenet' Reopen Theaters?
segments.note: the July 17 gamble and the domino effect

films.title: Tenet
films.note: The $200M Nolan/Pattinson original Warner Bros. wants as the first movie back. The plan reportedly needs 80% of the world's theaters open, including New York, L.A., and San Francisco.

films.title: Unhinged
films.note: The Russell Crowe thriller Sean fakes them out with before naming the real subject.

films.title: Trolls World Tour
films.note: One of the few releases still moving during the shutdown, via premium VOD.

films.title: The Wretched
films.note: IFC horror that made $85K across 21 drive-ins. Fine, but that wouldn't cover Tenet's catering.

films.title: Mulan
films.note: Another big-budget title dated soon after, needing repeat weekend viewings to make sense.

films.title: The Forever Purge
films.note: Blumhouse's fifth Purge, pulled off July 10 and pushed indefinitely. The pattern Sean expects to keep repeating.

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: The Aug. 14 date Tenet might slide into, which would bump WW84 to December.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: If Tenet moves, the Bond team starts weighing options too.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Cited as 'Top Gun 2,' then dated for Christmas: 'that would be a Merry Christmas for me.'

films.title: Dune
films.note: The other risky, semi-original tentpole they'd flagged for what already looked like a perilous year.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: Part of the winter slate that has to move if fall fills up first.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Also in the logjam of big winter films competing for a shrinking number of slots.

films.title: F9
films.note: Already moved a full year for audience safety: the precedent for why the Nolan movie can move too.

films.title: Bird Box
films.note: Their example of a movie the internet loved to unpack at home, whatever the critics thought.

films.title: The Lighthouse
films.note: The 'weird recent' Pattinson movie a Gen-Z viewer skips, versus his 'new flashy movies.'

films.title: Back to the Future
films.note: Zemeckis had only made three films when he made it; Sean's point that a studio couldn't hand that kind of budget to a newcomer today.

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: Spike Lee's Netflix film. Trailer dropped that morning, the kind of thing that keeps the show fed while theaters sit dark.

films.title: The King of Staten Island
films.note: 'I'd rather have it in my house than no movie at all.'

films.title: The Lovebirds
films.note: Pulled from Paramount's April theatrical slate to Netflix.

segments.heading: The Rest of the Movie Year
segments.note: lost festivals and a redefined Oscar

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Last year's Cannes debut, Palme d'Or, and Best Picture winner. Amanda's case: it won on a genius campaign as much as on being a great film.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Also opened at Cannes.

films.title: Burning
films.note: On Sean's short list of recent Cannes discoveries a canceled festival costs us.

films.title: Shoplifters
films.note: Kore-eda's Palme d'Or winner. His profile was lifted by a platform that may not exist this year.

films.title: Roma
films.note: Part of why American moviegoing has gone more global, alongside Netflix and Parasite.

films.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
films.note: Céline Sciamma already has an international following, one of the filmmakers who can survive without festivals.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: A New York Film Festival moment: the festival that inherits the burden if the fall circuit collapses.

films.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
films.note: Sean's example that there's no universal objective greatness: 'those people are nuts, but they believe it.'

segments.heading: In Memoriam
segments.note: Fred Willard, Lynn Shelton, Michel Piccoli

films.title: Best in Show
films.note: Both name Fred Willard's dog-show color commentary as their favorite of his performances.

films.title: Humpday
films.note: Lynn Shelton's mumblecore breakout out of the Swanberg/Gerwig moment, made after she came to filmmaking in her mid-30s.

films.title: Outside In
films.note: Her film with Edie Falco.

films.title: Sword of Trust
films.note: Her last film, one the show never covered, which Sean now regrets.

films.title: Contempt
films.note: Michel Piccoli, a familiar face of 20th-century European cinema.

films.title: Diary of a Chambermaid
films.note: One of his collaborations with Buñuel.

films.title: The Discreet Charm of the Bourgeoisie
films.note: Buñuel again.

films.title: Lady L
films.note: Alongside Peter Ustinov.

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

Portrait of a Lady on Fire.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire

The Discreet Charm of the Bourgeoisie.title: The Discreet Charm of the Bourgeoisie
